title: Loyalty by Edward Taylor — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
This is the hardest part:
When I came back to life
I was a good family dog
and not too friendly to strangers.
I got a thirty-five dollar raise
in salary, and through the pea-soup fogs
I drove the General, and introduced him
at rallies. I had a totalitarian approach
meaning: "Loyalty" by Edward Taylor is a nature poem consisting of 27 lines. The poet explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "This is the hardest part: When I came back to life...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edward Taylor's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
